description_long: This object is a large, irregularly shaped rock with a rough, angular form and a predominantly gray, speckled surface. The rock features a prominent raised section on one side, giving it a stepped or tiered appearance, while the rest of the surface is relatively flat but uneven. The texture is coarse, with visible striations and natural stone patterns, suggesting it is composed of granite or a similar hard stone. The coloration includes shades of gray, white, and subtle blueish tones, with no visible artificial markings or modifications. Its size and shape make it suitable as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban park or landscaped area. The rock appears solid and heavy, with a surface that could support weight but is not designed for comfort or detailed interaction.
description: A large, rough, gray stone with a stepped, angular form and a speckled, natural granite texture. It features a prominent raised section and a flat, uneven surface.
description_view_0: From this view, the rock's broad, flat face and the prominent raised section on the left are clearly visible.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the height and verticality of the raised section, with the flat expanse extending to the right.
description_view_2: The raised section appears centered, and the overall irregular, angular outline of the rock is most apparent.
description_view_3: The flat surface dominates, with the raised section now on the right, showing the rock's depth and layered structure.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
attribute_integration_with_urban_infrast: There is no visible evidence of intentional integration with urban infrastructure.
